question_title: How many maritime piracy & armed robbery incidents will the International Maritime Bureau report for Q1 2026?
summary: Q1 2026 IMB maritime piracy incidents forecast centered around 31 incidents (median). The 2025 surge was driven by Singapore Strait piracy (80 of 137 incidents), but Indonesian police arrested two gangs in July 2025, causing a structural break. Post-arrest quarters dropped sharply (Q3=26, Q4=21 vs Q1=45, Q2=45). Early 2026 ReCAAP data (6 incidents in ~19 days across Asia) confirms suppression is continuing but at a slightly higher rate than Q4 2025. Combined Bayesian projection from early data with a structural scenario model (allowing for possible piracy recovery) produces a right-skewed distribution with median ~31, well below the Q1 2025 value of 45 but above the post-arrest Q4 2025 value of 21.

factors.description: Indonesian police arrested two piracy gangs in July 2025, causing structural break in Singapore Strait incidents. Post-arrest quarters (Q3=26, Q4=21) were dramatically lower than pre-arrest (Q1=45, Q2=45).
ReCAAP reports only 6 incidents across all of Asia in first ~19 days of 2026, projecting to ~28-32 for full Q1 globally. Confirms suppression is ongoing.
Historical Q1 upward trend (27→33→45) suggests structural increase in piracy potential, even if temporarily suppressed by arrests.
Singapore Strait remains structurally attractive target for piracy (world's busiest shipping lane); new gangs could emerge. Recovery risk creates right tail.
Early 2026 daily rate (0.316/day) slightly higher than Q4 2025 rate (0.233/day), suggesting possible modest recovery from post-arrest lows.

reasoning:
Now let me search for more recent 2026 data from the IMB live map and any early 2026 reports.Let me try to get data from the IMB live map or other recent 2026 incident reports. Let me also look for IMO GISIS data.Very useful finding - Gard reports "Four incidents recorded January 1-27, 2026" for the Singapore Strait. Let me get more data on early 2026 incidents and also look at the "spike" article on Singapore Strait.Excellent - I found a key data point from ReCAAP: **6 incidents in Asia in 2026 so far** (as of around Jan 19, 2026). Also importantly, the Gard report mentions "Four incidents recorded January 1-27, 2026" for the Singapore Strait specifically. Now let me do the analysis.**Forecast: Q1 2026 IMB Maritime Piracy Incidents**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | 18 |
| 20th | 22 |
| 40th | 28 |
| **50th (median)** | **~31** |
| 60th | 34 |
| 80th | 43 |
| 90th | 51 |

**Key reasoning:**

1. **Structural break from arrests**: Indonesian police arrested two piracy gangs in July 2025, dropping quarterly incidents from 45 (Q1-Q2 avg) to 23.5 (Q3-Q4 avg). This is the dominant factor pulling the forecast below the Q1 2025 value of 45.

2. **Early 2026 data confirms suppression**: ReCAAP shows only 6 Asian incidents in the first ~19 days of 2026, projecting to ~28-32 for the full quarter globally. This is consistent with continued post-arrest suppression.

3. **Right skew from recovery risk**: The distribution is intentionally right-skewed (~16% probability above 45.5) because new piracy gangs could emerge in the Singapore Strait, which remains a structurally attractive target. The arrests addressed symptoms, not root causes.

4. **Below recent Q1 trend**: The 27→33→45 Q1 trend was accelerating, but the July 2025 arrests represent a clear intervention that broke this trajectory.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the number of maritime piracy and armed robbery incidents that take place during Q1 2026, according to the [International Maritime Bureau](https://icc-ccs.org/news/) (IMB) in its Q1 2026 quarterly report.
fine_print:
If the International Maritime Bureau does not release its Q1 2026 report or the data is otherwise unavailable before May 1, 2026, this question will be annulled.

This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42024) which opened on 2026-02-11 17:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print contained above. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
